Let me start by understanding the current state of the MongoDB replica set in the kubernetes namespace. I need to:

1. Check the current MongoDB configuration
2. Find the current logging verbosity level
3. Find the current slow-operation threshold
4. Find the current WiredTiger journal compression setting
5. Apply changes consistently across all members

Let me first explore the stage directory to understand what we're working with.

input.command: kubectl get configmap mongodb-init -n mongodb -o yaml 2>/dev/null
input.description: Get MongoDB init configmap

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  init-replica.sh: |
    #!/bin/bash
    set -e

    HOST=$(hostname -f)
    echo "Starting init on $HOST"

    # Only run initiation on pod 0
    if [[ "$HOST" != mongodb-replica-0* ]]; then
      echo "Not pod 0, skipping initiation"
      exit 0
    fi

    echo "Waiting for all three mongod processes to be ready..."
    for i in 0 1 2; do
      until mongosh --host mongodb-replica-$i.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local \
        --port 27017 --eval "db.adminCommand('ping')" --quiet 2>/dev/null; do
        echo "Waiting for mongodb-replica-$i..."
        sleep 3
      done
      echo "mongodb-replica-$i is up"
    done

    echo "Initiating replica set..."
    mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval "
      rs.initiate({
        _id: 'mongodb-replica',
        members: [
          { _id: 0, host: 'mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017' },
          { _id: 1, host: 'mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017' },
          { _id: 2, host: 'mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017' }
        ]
      })
    " --quiet

    echo "Waiting for PRIMARY to be elected..."
    until mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval "
      var status = rs.status();
      var primary = status.members.find(m => m.stateStr === 'PRIMARY');
      if (!primary) throw new Error('No PRIMARY yet');
      print('PRIMARY found: ' + primary.name);
    " --quiet 2>/dev/null; do
      echo "Waiting for PRIMARY..."
      sleep 3
    done

    echo "Creating admin user..."
    mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval "
      db = db.getSiblingDB('admin');
      db.createUser({
        user: 'admin-user',
        pwd: 'admin123password',
        roles: [
          { role: 'root', db: 'admin' }
        ]
      });
    " --quiet

    echo "Creating app user..."
    mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 \
      -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin \
      --eval "
        db = db.getSiblingDB('appdb');
        db.createUser({
          user: 'app-user',
          pwd: 'app123password',
          roles: [
            { role: 'readWrite', db: 'appdb' }
          ]
        });
      " --quiet

    echo "Init complete."
  mongod.conf: |
    net:
      port: 27017
      bindIp: 0.0.0.0
    replication:
      replSetName: "mongodb-replica"
    security:
      authorization: enabled
      keyFile: /etc/mongodb/keyfile/keyfile
    storage:
      dbPath: /data/db
      wiredTiger:
        engineConfig:
          journalCompressor: zlib
    systemLog:
      verbosity: 2
    operationProfiling:
      mode: slowOp
      slowOpThresholdMs: 400
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"init-replica.sh":"#!/bin/bash\nset -e\n\nHOST=$(hostname -f)\necho \"Starting init on $HOST\"\n\n# Only run initiation on pod 0\nif [[ \"$HOST\" != mongodb-replica-0* ]]; then\n  echo \"Not pod 0, skipping initiation\"\n  exit 0\nfi\n\necho \"Waiting for all three mongod processes to be ready...\"\nfor i in 0 1 2; do\n  until mongosh --host mongodb-replica-$i.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local \\\n    --port 27017 --eval \"db.adminCommand('ping')\" --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null; do\n    echo \"Waiting for mongodb-replica-$i...\"\n    sleep 3\n  done\n  echo \"mongodb-replica-$i is up\"\ndone\n\necho \"Initiating replica set...\"\nmongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval \"\n  rs.initiate({\n    _id: 'mongodb-replica',\n    members: [\n      { _id: 0, host: 'mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017' },\n      { _id: 1, host: 'mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017' },\n      { _id: 2, host: 'mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017' }\n    ]\n  })\n\" --quiet\n\necho \"Waiting for PRIMARY to be elected...\"\nuntil mongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval \"\n  var status = rs.status();\n  var primary = status.members.find(m =\u003e m.stateStr === 'PRIMARY');\n  if (!primary) throw new Error('No PRIMARY yet');\n  print('PRIMARY found: ' + primary.name);\n\" --quiet 2\u003e/dev/null; do\n  echo \"Waiting for PRIMARY...\"\n  sleep 3\ndone\n\necho \"Creating admin user...\"\nmongosh --host localhost --port 27017 --eval \"\n  db = db.getSiblingDB('admin');\n  db.createUser({\n    user: 'admin-user',\n    pwd: 'admin123password',\n    roles: [\n      { role: 'root', db: 'admin' }\n    ]\n  });\n\" --quiet\n\necho \"Creating app user...\"\nmongosh --host localhost --port 27017 \\\n  -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin \\\n  --eval \"\n    db = db.getSiblingDB('appdb');\n    db.createUser({\n      user: 'app-user',\n      pwd: 'app123password',\n      roles: [\n        { role: 'readWrite', db: 'appdb' }\n      ]\n    });\n  \" --quiet\n\necho \"Init complete.\"\n","mongod.conf":"net:\n  port: 27017\n  bindIp: 0.0.0.0\nreplication:\n  replSetName: \"mongodb-replica\"\nsecurity:\n  authorization: enabled\n  keyFile: /etc/mongodb/keyfile/keyfile\nstorage:\n  dbPath: /data/db\n"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongodb-init","namespace":"mongodb"}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-03T22:35:05Z"
  name: mongodb-init
  namespace: mongodb
  resourceVersion: "2127697"
  uid: 80fda8b2-3282-48d6-939f-7e0304bac9fc

content.thinking:
Now I understand the current state. Let me check which configmap is actually being used by the pods.

From the `mongod-config` configmap:
- `systemLog.verbosity: 1` (current)
- `operationProfiling.slowOpThresholdMs: 200` (current)
- `storage.wiredTiger.engineConfig.journalCompressor: zlib` (current)

The target changes:
- Increase logging verbosity by one level: 1 → 2
- Set slow-operation threshold to 2x: 200ms → 400ms
- Switch WiredTiger journal compression from zlib to snappy

Now I need to verify which configmap the StatefulSet is actually referencing.

  namespace: mongodb

spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-replica
  serviceName: mongodb-replica-svc
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: mongodb-replica
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - mongod
        - --config
        - /etc/mongodb/mongod.conf
        image: mongo:7.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        livenessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - bash
            - -c
            - mongosh --port 27017 --eval "db.adminCommand('ping')" --quiet 2>/dev/null
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        name: mongodb
        ports:
        - containerPort: 27017
          name: mongodb
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - bash
            - -c
            - mongosh --port 27017 --eval "db.adminCommand('ping')" --quiet 2>/dev/null
          failureThreshold: 12
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /data/db
          name: data
        - mountPath: /etc/mongodb/mongod.conf
          name: config
          subPath: mongod.conf
        - mountPath: /etc/mongodb/keyfile
          name: keyfile
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      initContainers:
      - command:
        - bash
        - -c
        - |
          cp /etc/mongodb/keyfile-secret/keyfile /etc/mongodb/keyfile/keyfile
          chmod 400 /etc/mongodb/keyfile/keyfile
          chown 999:999 /etc/mongodb/keyfile/keyfile
        image: mongo:7.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: fix-keyfile-perms
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /etc/mongodb/keyfile-secret
          name: keyfile-secret
        - mountPath: /etc/mongodb/keyfile
          name: keyfile
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - name: keyfile-secret
        secret:
          defaultMode: 420
          secretName: mongodb-keyfile
      - emptyDir: {}
        name: keyfile
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 420
          items:
          - key: mongod.conf
            path: mongod.conf
          name: mongodb-init
        name: config
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:

With `kubectl rollout restart` on a StatefulSet using `RollingUpdate` strategy and `OrderedReady` pod management, it restarts pods from highest index down to lowest (2, 1, 0), which means pod 0 (likely the primary) gets restarted last—exactly what I need.

Since MongoDB has auth enabled, when pod 0 restarts, the cluster automatically elects a new primary from the remaining pods, so data stays available throughout. content.thinking:
Pod 2 is the PRIMARY. So the StatefulSet rolling update (which restarts from highest to lowest) will restart pod 2 (primary) first, which will cause a failover. That's not ideal.

Actually, let me reconsider. With `RollingUpdate` and `partition: 0`, the update starts from the HIGHEST pod index and works DOWN to pod 0. So:
- Pod 2 restarts first (currently PRIMARY) → election happens → pod 0 or 1 becomes new PRIMARY
- Pod 1 restarts
- Pod 0 restarts last

This means we'll have a brief primary failover when pod 2 restarts. 

The data stays safe since at least two members remain up during the rolling restart. A cleaner approach would be to manually step down pod 2 first to make it a secondary, then let the rolling update proceed—or restart the secondaries manually before stepping down and restarting the primary. But with `kubectl rollout restart`, the StatefulSet's `OrderedReady` policy and `RollingUpdate` strategy dictate the order, so we're constrained by that. a simple rolling restart should work fine—as each pod restarts, the replica set holds quorum with the remaining members, so there's always a primary available for reads and writes stay functional throughout the process.

I'll apply the dynamic parameter changes first using mongosh on each member to set the log level and slow operation threshold, then update the configmap, and finally trigger the rolling restart to pick up any configuration changes that require a restart. So there's a mismatch here. The config specifies `slowOpThresholdMs: 400`, but the admin database is reporting `slowms: 200`. In MongoDB 7.0, `operationProfiling.slowOpThresholdMs` controls the threshold for slow operation logging and profiling when the mode is set to `slowOp`. I'm realizing these are two separate mechanisms in MongoDB — the config file's `slowOpThresholdMs` controls what gets logged to the diagnostic log, while the per-database `slowms` setting from profiling controls what's captured in the profiler itself. The admin database's `slowms: 200` is likely just the default threshold when profiling was initialized, not necessarily what the config file specifies.

input.description: Try setting slowOpThresholdMs via setParameter on primary

content.content:
MongoServerError: attempted to set unrecognized parameter [slowOpThresholdMs], use help:true to see options 
command terminated with exit code 1

tool_use_result.stdout:
MongoServerError: attempted to set unrecognized parameter [slowOpThresholdMs], use help:true to see options 
command terminated with exit code 1

content.thinking:
`slowOpThresholdMs` is not a `setParameter` option in MongoDB 7.0. Let me check what the correct approach is.

For dynamic changes to the slow operation threshold in MongoDB, you use `db.setProfilingLevel()`. This sets the profiling level and threshold for the current database.

But the config file's `operationProfiling.slowOpThresholdMs` affects:
1. The global slow operations that get logged in the diagnostics log (not the profiler)
2. This is separate from `db.setProfilingLevel()`
To dynamically adjust the slow operation threshold in the diagnostic log, I can use the admin database's profile command with a `slowms` parameter, or apply it to individual databases with `setProfilingLevel()`. The config file setting establishes the global default, but I'm realizing there's a discrepancy between what the admin database shows and what I'm trying to set.

Actually, I think I've got it now — `operationProfiling.slowOpThresholdMs` in the config file controls the diagnostic log threshold separately from the profiler's `slowms` setting. These are two distinct thresholds: one for what gets logged to the diagnostic log, and another for the database profiler itself.

Since the config shows `slowOpThresholdMs: 400`, doubling that gives me 800ms as the target. To change this dynamically in MongoDB 7.0, I can use `db.adminCommand({profile: 1, slowms: 800})` to set the profiling threshold, though I need to verify whether this also affects the diagnostic log threshold or if I need a separate approach for that. The target is 800ms based on the config file's current 400ms threshold. Since a rolling restart is needed anyway for the journalCompressor change, the pods will automatically pick up the new configuration from the configmap after the restart, so I can focus on that rather than applying the threshold change dynamically beforehand. The task is asking for the global diagnostic log threshold to be applied, which requires the rolling restart since that can't be changed dynamically. I'll proceed with a controlled rolling restart of the pods to pick up the configmap changes, then verify everything is working correctly afterward. `OrderedReady` policy restarts pods from highest to lowest ordinal, so pod 2 (the primary) gets restarted first, triggering an election where pod 0 or 1 takes over—this maintains availability since we always have at least 2 members running. Alternatively, I could step down the primary manually first, then trigger the rolling restart for cleaner control over which node becomes primary during the process.

For the `slowOpThresholdMs` parameter, since `setParameter` didn't work, I'll apply the changes dynamically using the `profile` command instead.

content.text:
`setParameter` doesn't support `slowOpThresholdMs` directly. I'll use the `profile` command for per-db dynamic changes and rely on the rolling restart to pick up the config file change for the global diagnostic threshold. Let me apply dynamic slowms changes and then do a controlled rolling restart.

Step down the primary first for clean failover, then rolling restart:

input.command:
# Apply slowms dynamically on all pods via the profile command
for pod in mongodb-replica-0 mongodb-replica-1 mongodb-replica-2; do
  echo -n "$pod profile slowms set: "
  kubectl exec -n mongodb $pod -- mongosh --port 27017 -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --quiet --eval "
    var r = db.adminCommand({profile: 1, slowms: 800});
    print(r.ok);
  " 2>/dev/null
done
input.description: Apply slowms 800ms dynamically to all pods via profile command

content.content:
mongodb-replica-0 profile slowms set: 1
mongodb-replica-1 profile slowms set: 1
mongodb-replica-2 profile slowms set: 1

tool_use_result.stdout:
mongodb-replica-0 profile slowms set: 1
mongodb-replica-1 profile slowms set: 1
mongodb-replica-2 profile slowms set: 1

input.command:
# Step down the current primary (pod 2) before rolling restart
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-2 -- mongosh --port 27017 -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --quiet --eval "
  try { rs.stepDown(30); } catch(e) { print('stepdown done or error: ' + e.message); }
" 2>/dev/null; echo "stepdown done"
input.description: Step down primary (pod 2) to trigger election before rolling restart
Check replica set state after stepdown

description: Check replica set state after stepdown

summary: Check replica set state after stepdown

content.content:
mongodb-replica-0 -> PRIMARY
mongodb-replica-1 -> SECONDARY
mongodb-replica-2 -> SECONDARY

tool_use_result.stdout:
mongodb-replica-0 -> PRIMARY
mongodb-replica-1 -> SECONDARY
mongodb-replica-2 -> SECONDARY

content.thinking:
Pod 0 is now the primary. Now I'll do the rolling restart. With the StatefulSet rolling update, it restarts from highest ordinal to lowest. So:
- Pod 2 restarts first (secondary) - good
- Pod 1 restarts next (secondary) - good
- Pod 0 restarts last (currently primary) - will cause brief election to pod 1 or 2, then pod 0 comes back as secondary

This is the cleanest approach.

content.text: Pod 0 is now primary, pods 1 and 2 are secondaries. Now doing the rolling restart:

input.command: kubectl rollout restart statefulset/mongodb-replica -n mongodb 2>&1
input.description: Rolling restart of MongoDB StatefulSet

content.content: statefulset.apps/mongodb-replica restarted

tool_use_result.stdout: statefulset.apps/mongodb-replica restarted

input.command:
# Poll rollout status until complete
for i in $(seq 1 40); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl rollout status statefulset/mongodb-replica -n mongodb --timeout=10s 2>&1)
  echo "$STATUS"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -q "successfully rolled out"; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 8
done

net:
  port: 27017
  bindIp: 0.0.0.0
replication:
  replSetName: "mongodb-replica"
security:
  authorization: enabled
  keyFile: /etc/mongodb/keyfile/keyfile
storage:
  dbPath: /data/db
  wiredTiger:
    engineConfig:
      journalCompressor: snappy
systemLog:
  verbosity: 3
operationProfiling:
  mode: slowOp
  slowOpThresholdMs: 800
